rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CHRISTINE YOUNG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Preliminary Tentative Ruling (subject to change): Hearing. Appearance required. The following issues to be cleared: (1) Notice of Petition to Administer Estate [Form DE-121] must be given to (1) the non-petitioning heir (Teal Jaa) and (2) who filed Request for Special Notices the following persons (Paul F. Utrecht, Esq. counsel for Mark Freyman and Dawn Freyman - filed on 4/22/2016 and Kurt Rifbjerg, Esq. - filed 6/29/2023). Prob. Code sec. 8522(b) (2) Duties and Liabilities of Personal Representative [Form DE-147] to be filed. Prob. Code sec. 8404. Local Rule 14.18.E. Need the proposed successor administrator's contact information to review her/his consent to act and to determine whether any minimum bond for a non CA resident fiduciary is needed. (3) Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number [Form DE-147S] to be filed. (4) Petition #5.a.(3)-(4) to set forth whether the decedent was survived by registered domestic partner or not. (5) Petition #5.a.(7)-(8) to set forth whether the decedent was survived issue of a predeceased child or not. (6) Proposed Order to be submitted. Local Rule 14.7. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FRANCES JOSEPHINE THOMPSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: Verified Petition To Determine Heirship And Entitlement To Estate
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/20/24. No earlier available date. 1) Not all heirs who are now purportedly the petitioners have consented in writing to the dual representation by the attorneys, who originally represented American Research Bureau. In addition to Patricia Louise Ritchie [Ripley], no consents have been filed by Russel[l] Patrick Ritchie, who reportedly passed away (although the date of his death is not disclosed and his successor-in-interest has not been identified) or by Marc Maxwell. 2) Joan Morrow signed the consent on behalf of Tom Morrow, whom she reports as having predeceased Decedent on 12/5/13. Who are his successors-in-interest? 3) The verification attached to the attorney's "Updated Declaration" filed on 5/7/24 shows the signatory as "Robert Christopher Ripley" and Robert Christopher Ritchie. 4) No proof of service of the notice of hearing with the correct, current instructions for how to appear remotely has been filed yet. The proof of service filed on 5/7/24 showed that notice was mailed on 5/7/24 for a hearing on 5/8/24. (Despite petitioners' attorney's assertion that the five individuals originally thought to be paternal heirs are not actually heirs, notice was still given to them?) 5) The copy of post-deceased heir John M. Ritchie's will is not necessarily proof of his successor-in-interest. 6) Theodore Ritchie's daughter, Laura E. Ritchie Jewell, reportedly died without issue, but her death certificate, attached to the attorney's "Updated Declaration" filed on 5/7/24 shows the informant as her son, David Rhae Alcala. Deadline is 11/8/24 for providing a revised proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF IRENE CHRISTINE SANDVIK
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, as no ex parte petition has been filed to request an order extending special administration, and nothing has been filed since the previous two hearings to address the following issues (initially e-mailed to counsel on 04/10/2024 and subsequently posted as tentative rulings): 1) No DE-121 form is on file. A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the form at least 15 days before the hearing. Notice must also be given to the Norwegian Consulate, if it appears that property will pass to citizens of Norway. See Probate Code section 8113. 2) No proof of publication is on file. 3) Item 6d of the petition must be checked, if the decedent was survived by issue of first cousins (including first cousins once removed or twice removed). 4) The personal representative of the estate of Klara Hauglis must be identified as such by a verified allegation, and notice must be given. If there is no personal representative, all heirs of Klara if she died intestate (who may include Klara's surviving spouse if she was married), or devisees if Klara died testate, must be identified as such by a verified allegation, and notice must be given to each heir or devisee. 5) Direct notice must be given to Turid Bjelland, absent a signed directive stating that notice should be given in care of Turid's spouse. CRC 7.51(a)(2). 6) A declaration required by CRC 7.52(a) must be filed if Else Mundal's address remains unknown. Whether notice can be prescribed in any manner other than mailing (e.g., telephone, e-mail, social media, etc.) must also be addressed. See CRC 7.52(c), section 1212, and CCP section 413.30. 7) Each factor under section 8465(d)(1)-(4) must be addressed as it applies to the petitioner as nominee of a foreign heir. NOTE: Item 8 and Attachment 8 of the petition must be supplemented to add issue of paternal grandparents identified by their assignee or separate counsel of record. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ZETTIE BERNETT HIGHTOWER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Jennifer Hightower's Petition is continued to 11/20/24. (No earlier date available.) (1) Notice of Hearing must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as information about remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (2) Items 2d and 3e of the petition are silent regarding bond. If not all heirs waive bond, and the petitioner seeks full IAEA authority, bond will be for the full estimated value of the estate. (3) Item 5b of the petition must be answered. (4) The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially with a cover sheet. Deadline is 11/8/24 for providing courtesy copies to the assigned examiner. A supplement to the petition, signed and verified by the petitioner, must be filed to address the missing information in her petition. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ZETTIE BERNETT HIGHTOWER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Tiana Blunt's Amended Petition is continued to 11/20/24. (No earlier date available.) (1) There was no "Legal Statement" attached to the attachment to the amended petition. (2) Mailed notice must have attached to it the CURRENT instructions for how to make a remote appearance as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. (3) Published notice does not require these instructions to be included; however the wrong instructions were also published, so published notice is also incorrect. Deadline is 11/8/24 for providing courtesy copies to the assigned examiner. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MICHAEL A GRISHAVER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/23/24 to be heard with the competing petition, although neither appears to be procedurally ready to be heard. Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) No proof of service or proof of publication of notice has been filed. (3) Mailed notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (4) The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially with a cover sheet. (5) The petition caption is incomplete if the petitioner is seeking full IAEA authority. (6) Item 8 of the petition does not show the relationship to the decedent of each person listed. (7) If an heir is deceased, their date of death must be set forth. [LRSF 14.18 C. and D.] (8) Item of the petition must also clearly identify the trustee of the devisee trust. If the petitioner is the trustee, all beneficiaries of the trust must be listed, clearly identified as such, and they must receive notice. (9) If the addresses of the alternate executors are unknown, the petitioner must explain efforts she made to locate them. If, after due diligence, she is still unable to locate them, this Court requires an ex parte petition to dispense with notice to anyone who is entitled to notice. Deadline is 9/13/24 for providing the assigned examiner a proposed order and courtesy copies of the petition and any papers filed to address these issues. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF VIRGINIA CORPUZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing Status of filing the Inventory and Appraisal will be set for 1/21/25. If the I&A is not filed before that date, an appearance will be required on 1/21/25. Status of closing the estate will be set for 9/30/25. The administrator must file either a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report of status of administration pursuant to Prob. C. Section 12200 (requesting additional time for estate administration) at least two weeks before that status date. Appearance will be required on 9/30/25 if no petition has been filed.
